content.text: open standard for connecting AI models to external tools and data
both remote and local MCP servers
: local servers running on your machine (Node, Python, custom binaries) as well as cloud‑hosted services.
Typical local MCP servers on desktop:
– lets Claude read and write local files, navigate project directories, and understand your codebase structure.
– interact with repositories (read PRs, review diffs, create issues, search commits).
– drive a real browser for scraping, filling forms, and generating screenshots.
– connect to local DBs, Brave Search, or other dev tools for richer context.
This effectively turns Claude Desktop into a
that can inspect and modify your actual files and tools rather than just working on paste‑ins.
